title: Licensing & provenance framework (OFL.txt / FONTLOG.txt / provenance.json + clean-room policy)
context: open-accessible-fonts is OFL-only. Before any glyph work begins, the project must establish a clean-room policy, provenance model, and licensing framework that makes the output legally redistributable globally. This is a foundational governance document task that is partner-independent and can proceed immediately.
objective: Define and document the project's licensing framework: OFL.txt and FONTLOG.txt templates with Reserved Font Name policy, a provenance.json schema for per-glyph-source traceability, a clean-room policy with prohibited-inputs allow/deny list, and an output-license matrix covering fonts (OFL-1.1), code (MIT), and docs/data (CC-BY-4.0).
acceptanceCriteria: OFL.txt and a populated FONTLOG.txt template are included with Reserved Font Name policy documented.
provenance.json schema is defined with per-glyph-source granularity, recording: origin, license, designer, references, date, reviewer, community sign-off.

Clean-room policy documents the allowed input allow-list (original clean-room, OFL derivatives, CC0/PD references) and the prohibited-inputs deny-list (proprietary tracing/autotracing, unknown-provenance sources).
Output-license matrix is documented: OFL-1.1 for fonts, MIT for code/tooling, CC-BY-4.0 for docs/data/specimens.
Policy explains that US typeface-design copyright carve-out is deliberately not relied upon; the stricter clean-room standard applies for global redistribution safety.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys are included in any committed document.
output: A document (or set of committed files) establishing the provenance.json schema, OFL.txt/FONTLOG.txt templates, clean-room policy, prohibited-inputs list, and output-license matrix, merged as a PR to the repository.
